read.sdma_definition: SDMA is MORI's DMA-engine-backed all-reduce path, enabled here through mori.ccl.AllreduceSdma and MORI_ENABLE_SDMA=1.
read.what_materialized: The clean six-step run is stable and confirms the SDMA backend can be used in the MORI/AITER hot-weight backward path without timing sidecars.
read.what_did_not_materialize: The sampled W13/W2 all-reduce speedup does not become e2e throughput uplift. The run is slightly below the same route-mask/min-helper branch using the default reduce path and far below the standard-EP top-8 candidate.
read.likely_reason: Hot-weight dW reduction is only one microstage. The route-mask MORI/AITER path is still dominated by helper integration overhead, tiny helper AITER calls, owner local VJP, combine wait, and route-mask planning/masking.
read.decision: Keep mori_sdma as a valid backend option, but do not promote the route-mask SDMA branch. The next materialization work should make helper hot-row execution fit the normal MORI dispatch plus AITER fused expert path, or overlap/chunk SDMA with owner/helper VJP instead of only replacing a blocking all-reduce.
